label: System Health
description: Service health grid showing operational status of platform services with latency and error indicators. Preview on overview shares the same hook (useServiceHealth → /api/service-status/health) as the full observe/health page; widget renders the entire service list inside a scrollable container with a Refresh action.

0.5.note: No lib/mocks/ imports in widget file.

0.9.note: No dead code; refresh handler is wired (queryClient.invalidateQueries).

l0.notes: All 9 checks pass after 2026-04-25 update (refresh button + full-list scroll).

1.3.note: Service health is infrastructure-level, not org-scoped by design.

1.6.note: No scope filtering — full services list shown.

1.7.note: Preview now hydrates from /api/service-status/health (same source as observe/health page); SEED_SERVICES retained only as the fallback constant in lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts when the API hook returns nothing.

l1.notes: All checks pass. 1.3/1.4/1.5 N/A: infrastructure-level data.

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab and f9f29ea.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ batch ✓ paper ✓ — display-only, mode-agnostic.

3.2.note: Context reads useGlobalScope(); health data via useServiceHealth().

3.4.note: No mode indicator needed; platform health is mode-agnostic.

3.5.note: Paper mode: identical render — platform services run independently of trading mode.

3.6.note: No WebSocket/interval inside the widget; useServiceHealth polls every 30s in the context producer.

l3.notes: Agent checks 3.1/3.2/3.5/3.6 pass. 3.3/3.4 require human walkthrough.

4.1.note: Refresh button onClick → queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['service-health']}). View All Button wrapped in Link. No dead handlers.

4.4.note: Refresh click verified by unit test (Refresh button invalidates the service-health query). Visual confirmation captured 2026-04-25 via Playwright on /services/trading/overview — all 12 services render with proper freshness/SLA values and ok/lag statuses.

4.6.note: No entitlement checks in render path.

4.8.note: Body wrapped in flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto so the full service list scrolls within the widget bounds at default 6×3 size; no overflow leaking.

5.1.note: Target strategies: N/A — health-grid shows platform infrastructure health, applicable across all trading modes as an operational support widget.

l5.notes: 5.1 agent-prep done. 5.2–5.6 N/A — infrastructure-monitoring, not strategy-specific.

l6.notes: 8 tests passing in tests/widgets/overview/health-grid.test.tsx: service rows render, table columns, full-list rendering (no preview cap), loading spinner, empty-state, Refresh button invalidates ['service-health'] query, View All link target, null-context fallback. Tests wrap the widget in a QueryClientProvider so useQueryClient resolves.

7.2.note: No lib/mocks imports in widget file.

7.3.note: bottom-widgets.tsx is 200 lines; HealthGridWidget is ~40 lines. Under all limits.

7.5.note: No datetime rendering inside this widget — no toLocale*/Date toString calls.

7.7.note: No useEffect. handleRefresh wrapped in useCallback([queryClient]); handleSort in useCallback([sortKey,sortDir]); sortedServices in useMemo([allMockServices,sortKey,sortDir]). All hooks called unconditionally above the ctx early return.

l7.notes: All L7 checks pass after 2026-04-25 update.

8.2.note: grep '[pm][lrtbxy]?-\[|gap-\[' → zero matches. New classes (gap-1, gap-1.5, p-3, mb-2, flex-1, min-h-0, overflow-y-auto) all use scale tokens.

8.3.note: Uses Button, Spinner, Link, RefreshCw (lucide), HealthStatusGrid — all shared primitives.

8.4.note: Agent: Refresh and View All are proper

keepSeparate.rationale: Infrastructure health is mode- and scope-independent; it stands alone as a global platform status view. Shares hook + endpoint with the full observe/health page so both surfaces stay in sync.

relationships.notes: Infrastructure-level health — not coupled to trading scope or strategy selection. Refresh action invalidates the same React Query key as the full page so both refresh together.

dataContract.mockSource: lib/api/mock-handler.ts (/api/service-status/health) — same mock as observe/health page; SEED_SERVICES in lib/mocks/fixtures/trading-pages.ts retained only as fallback when the API hook returns nothing.
dataContract.notes: Service health fetched via useServiceHealth() with 30s refetchInterval. Refresh button invokes queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['service-health']}); the same query is consumed by /services/observe/health, so a refresh on either surface updates both. Preview renders the entire service list in a scrollable container; full page exposes additional columns (tier, latencyP50/99, uptime, version, cpu/memory, etc.).

gaps: Health polling interval not configurable (hardcoded 30s in useServiceHealth)
coverage.notes: Infrastructure health is platform-wide. Preview now renders the same full service set as the observe/health page (no preview cap); column subset is intentional — preview shows Service / Freshness / SLA / Status, full page exposes the rest.

findings.fix: Added coreLoading Spinner guard before HealthStatusGrid render
Added 'No services reported' guard on allMockServices.length===0

findings.description: HealthGridWidget — ~3 user-visible strings not i18n wrapped
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21

findings.description: /api/service-status/health mock returned services without freshness/sla — preview cast to ServiceHealth, so columns rendered '--' and getStatus() fell through to STALE
findings.fix: Added freshness + sla numeric fields to every service in lib/api/mock-handler.ts so the same mock now drives the preview widget and the observe/health page consistently

findings.description: Preview hard-sliced to 6 services; user requested full list with column subset
findings.fix: Removed slice; wrapped HealthStatusGrid in flex-1 min-h-0 overflow-y-auto so the full service list scrolls inside the widget

findings.description: No refresh affordance on the preview widget — users had to navigate to the full page to force a refetch
findings.fix: Added Refresh button (lucide RefreshCw) left of View All; onClick calls queryClient.invalidateQueries({queryKey:['service-health']}), which is the same key consumed by the observe/health page

findings.description: Test suite asserted 'caps rendered services at 6' which no longer matches behavior, and lacked QueryClientProvider needed by the new useQueryClient call
findings.fix: Replaced the cap test with full-list rendering assertion, added a Refresh-invalidates-query test, wrapped renders in QueryClientProvider; 8/8 tests passing

findings.description: HealthGridWidget: useState/useCallback/useMemo called after 'if (!ctx) return' early return — Rules of Hooks violation causing sort state to be silently discarded on every render; default health-priority sort had no effect
findings.fix: Moved all hooks (useState, useCallback, useMemo) above the early return; replaced ctx destructuring with optional-chaining fallbacks (ctx?.allMockServices ?? []) so hooks can be called unconditionally

findings.description: Sortable HealthDataTable added — column headers sortable by click; default sort floats unhealthy services (critical=0, warning=1, live=2, idle=3) to top
findings.fix: Replaced HealthStatusGrid with custom HealthDataTable sub-component; getHealthOrder() drives default status sort; sortedServices useMemo handles per-column sort state; row highlighting via getRowClass (warning/critical CSS vars)
